justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime dual-write strategy clearly designed with outbox pattern; (2) Phases clearly defined — backfill (Phase 1), dual-write (Phase 2), read cutover (Phase 3), decommission (Phase 4); (3) Rollback plan via feature flag controller with instant phase rollback, explicitly noting Phase 3→2 safety because dual-write keeps MongoDB current; (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified as 'critical' severity — mentions nested arrays, null/missing fields, polymorphic structures, and requires schema mapping tests before backfill; (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency (~5-15ms), operational burden of maintaining two stores, and code complexity in the repository abstraction layer.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed phase-by-phase migration plan with clear timeline (6-8 weeks) and specific phase transition criteria
Backfill risk thoroughly addressed: 8M records estimated at ~13 hours, batch size 1000-5000, watermark for resume, COPY for bulk inserts, off-peak scheduling
Writes during backfill handled correctly — backfill must complete before dual-write begins for a given date range, explicitly called out as a pitfall to avoid
MongoDB document shape risk rated 'critical' with concrete mitigation: sample 10k random docs, test round-trip fidelity, include edge cases (0 items, 100+ items, null fields)
Rollback safety well-designed: Phase 4 only entered after 2+ weeks stable Phase 3 with zero verification discrepancies; premature MongoDB removal explicitly warned against
Verification Service as shadow-read comparator is a strong addition beyond basic dual-write — provides confidence signal before cutover
Multiple alternatives considered for each trade-off decision (CDC/Debezium, big-bang, JSONB, database-level replication) with clear rejection rationale
Confidence at 78 is reasonable but could be higher given the thoroughness of the design — the low-confidence areas are honest but the mitigations are solid
